sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and crime, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper (1997) is best described as Animation, Crime, Mystery, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Time Bombed Skyscraper part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
